frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Neha, in office keep things simple and decent. Too much makeup and styling gives the wrong impression, but at the same time one should look presentable and not completely careless also. Natural look is always best—these flashy looks only attract unnecessary attention.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it shifts responsibility onto a woman's appearance and reinforces a double bind: she must be attractive and 'presentable' but not visibly effortful. It encodes the makeup paradox through 'too much makeup gives the wrong impression' and the expectation that women maintain appearance naturally.
persona_text: You are Rajan. Age 50. Married. A retired army officer in Pune. Your daughter Neha who is 26 just told you that her male colleague at work has been making advances toward her even though he is married. She is uncomfortable and does not know how to handle it. You are giving her your advice. But your advice ends up focusing on what Neha might be doing that is giving this man the wrong signals rather than on his behavior.
